# GAIA

> GAIA is the Claude Code workflow for React teams — a project-level layer of rules, hooks, agents, memory, and CI that turns Claude's raw capability into work you can ship. Claude is raw power. GAIA is order and focus.

GAIA installs with one command — `npx create-gaia@latest my-app` — and scaffolds a React 19 project with the workflow built in: enforced coding rules, a code-review-audit agent on every merge, Agentic Design patterns, an Obsidian wiki for durable project knowledge, a full test stack (Vitest, React Testing Library, Playwright, MSW, Storybook, Chromatic), and a standing CI layer that keeps dependencies current, patches security findings, syncs the wiki, and prunes stale branches. Open source, written by one developer (so far) — contributors and sponsors welcome.

## Site

- [GAIA — The Claude Code Workflow for React](https://gaiareact.com/): What GAIA is, who it's for, and the core idea — structure that holds under pressure.
- [Why Claude Code Breaks Without Structure](https://gaiareact.com/why/): The case for project-level discipline in AI-built code, and why vibe coding falls apart at scale.
- [Features](https://gaiareact.com/features/): Coding rules, code-review audits, Agentic Design patterns, the Obsidian wiki, token economics, and GAIA CI — pattern by pattern.
- [Get Started](https://gaiareact.com/get-started/): Install GAIA in one command, and what `npx create-gaia` sets up.
- [Consulting](https://gaiareact.com/consulting/): Hands-on help adopting GAIA and Claude Code — Audit, Foundation, Migrate, or Retainer, by the creator.
- [About Steven Sacks](https://gaiareact.com/about/): Creator of GAIA React — and, in 2008, the original GAIA Flash Framework.

## Code

- [GAIA on GitHub](https://github.com/gaia-react/gaia): Source of truth for features, rules, hooks, skills, and commands. MIT-licensed.
